propertyRatesAiData.marketSnapshotOverview:
Delhi's property market presents a dynamic landscape with an average asking price of ₹18,190 per sq ft. The city's real estate has shown consistent growth over the past year, making it an attractive hub for both buyers and investors. The rental market also appears robust, with an average rental rate of ₹37 per sq ft and a healthy rental yield of 2.44%, indicating good returns for landlords.
  • The average asking price in Delhi has climbed steadily, reaching ₹17,789 per sq ft by Dec 2025, up from ₹8,753 per sq ft in Mar 2025.
  • West Delhi stands out with a remarkable 48.52% increase in asking prices, averaging ₹16,821 per sq ft.
  • Shops command the highest average asking price at ₹1.2 Lakh per sq ft, demonstrating a significant 94.2% growth.
  • Central Delhi leads the rental micromarkets with an average rate of ₹47 per sq ft, showing a 4.44% increase.
  • New Launch properties, though limited in units, have seen a substantial 64.57% surge in average price.
propertyRatesAiData.insightsLong:
Delhi's property market showcases a vibrant and growing ecosystem, with the average asking price currently standing at ₹18,190 per sq ft. A review of quarterly price trends reveals consistent upward momentum, as rates have steadily climbed from ₹8,753 per sq ft in March 2025 to ₹17,789 per sq ft by December 2025, reflecting strong market confidence. The rental sector complements this growth, offering an average rental rate of ₹37 per sq ft and an appealing rental yield of 2.44%, making it an attractive proposition for investors. Micromarkets like West Delhi and South Delhi have been particularly dynamic, alongside significant price appreciation in specific property types.
  • The city's average property rates have nearly doubled in a year, moving from ₹8,753 per sq ft in Mar 2025 to ₹17,789 per sq ft in Dec 2025.
  • West Delhi experienced the most significant growth among micromarkets, with asking prices soaring by 48.52% to ₹16,821 per sq ft.
  • The shop property segment recorded an impressive 94.2% price increase, reaching ₹1.2 Lakh per sq ft, highlighting strong commercial demand.
  • Under Construction projects show a positive trend with a 13.11% price increase, averaging ₹16,883 per sq ft, indicating builder confidence.
  • The average rent for a 4 BHK apartment reaches ₹2.03 Lakh per month, underscoring the premium residential rental market.
propertyRatesAiData.insightsShort: Delhi's property market is experiencing significant growth, with the average asking price at ₹18,190 per sq ft and a strong upward trend observed over the past year. The rental market also performs well, boasting an average rate of ₹37 per sq ft and a healthy 2.44% rental yield. Noteworthy gains are seen in West Delhi and the shop property segment, indicating robust demand.
propertyRatesAiData.askingPriceTrendsDescription: Delhi's property market has demonstrated a remarkable upward trajectory in asking prices over the past year. Rates have consistently risen, starting from ₹8,753 per sq ft in March 2025. This growth continued through June 2025, reaching ₹11,983 per sq ft, and further accelerated to ₹16,375 per sq ft by September 2025, culminating at ₹17,789 per sq ft by December 2025. This sustained increase highlights a strong and confident market sentiment.
propertyRatesAiData.byAreaDescription: Delhi's micromarkets present a varied pricing landscape, catering to diverse investment profiles. Central Delhi commands the highest average rate at ₹25,406 per sq ft, despite a -6.09% change, showcasing its premium positioning. South Delhi closely follows at ₹23,695 per sq ft, experiencing robust growth of 12.5%. West Delhi has seen a phenomenal surge, with rates averaging ₹16,821 per sq ft after an impressive 48.52% increase. Meanwhile, Dwarka offers properties at ₹14,963 per sq ft with a modest 0.85% rise, while North Delhi and East Delhi provide more accessible options at ₹12,235 per sq ft and ₹11,815 per sq ft respectively, with East Delhi registering a 4.1% increase.
propertyRatesAiData.ratesByPropertyTypesDescription: Delhi's real estate market caters to diverse buyer preferences across various property categories. Commercial shops lead the premium segment, commanding an impressive ₹1.2 Lakh per sq ft and experiencing a substantial 94.2% growth. Villas also represent a high-value residential option, priced at ₹57,762 per sq ft with a healthy 36.33% increase. Apartments, the most common residential type, average ₹18,190 per sq ft and show a modest 2.25% rise, while office spaces are available at ₹12,088 per sq ft, though they have seen a -22.65% change.
propertyRatesAiData.ratesByProjectStatusDescription: The Delhi market offers properties across various development stages, catering to different buyer timelines and preferences. Ready To Move inventory, comprising 1175 units, averages ₹14,345 per sq ft, though it has seen a -5.12% change, appealing to immediate occupancy seekers. Under Construction projects, with 11 units, present opportunities at ₹16,883 per sq ft and have experienced a positive 13.11% price increase. New Launch projects, though only 2 units, show a significant 64.57% price surge at ₹9,937 per sq ft, indicating high future potential. Well Occupied properties are priced at ₹13,644 per sq ft with a -1.86% change.
propertyRatesAiData.topProjectsAskingDescription: Premium residential developments in Delhi are commanding significant asking prices, reflecting their luxury positioning and prime locations. Leading the segment, Godrej Connaught One in Connaught Place, Central Delhi, is priced at ₹80,393 per sq ft, setting a high-value benchmark. Boutique Residential Apartments C-253 in Defence Colony, South Delhi, follows with ₹59,012 per sq ft, showing a substantial 62.77% growth. Kailash Nath Som Vihar in Rama Krishna Puram, South Delhi, offers properties at ₹50,399 per sq ft with a 30.06% increase. Several RWA projects in Dilshad Garden and Defence Colony also feature prominently, showcasing consistent rates around ₹40,849 per sq ft.
investmentOpportunities: Investing in West Delhi micromarket properties could yield significant returns, given its remarkable 48.52% increase in asking prices.
The shop property segment shows robust growth with a 94.2% increase in average asking price, presenting a strong commercial investment opportunity.
New Launch properties, despite limited units, have seen a substantial 64.57% price surge, indicating high appreciation potential for early investors.
The city's rental yield of 2.44% indicates a healthy return on investment for rental properties.
Showrooms in Delhi offer high rental income potential, averaging ₹173 per sq ft with a 19.31% increase.
Under Construction projects, with a 13.11% price increase, represent an opportunity for capital appreciation upon completion.
marketChallenges: The office space property type has experienced a significant -22.65% change in asking price, suggesting potential headwinds in this segment.
Central Delhi's asking prices have seen a -6.09% change, indicating a recent cooling in this otherwise premium micromarket.
Ready To Move properties have seen a -5.12% change in average price, which might reflect a shift in buyer preference or increased supply.
Apartment rental rates have seen a -5.13% change, suggesting a slight dip in rental income potential for this popular residential type.
East Delhi's rental rates have decreased by -7.14%, potentially impacting rental income for investors in that area.
marketStrengths: Delhi's overall asking price trend shows consistent quarterly growth from Mar 2025 to Dec 2025, indicating a strong and stable market.
West Delhi and South Delhi micromarkets exhibit significant growth in asking prices (48.52% and 12.5% respectively), highlighting their investment appeal.
The shop property type leads with an impressive 94.2% price increase, demonstrating strong demand and value for commercial real estate.
A healthy rental yield of 2.44% positions Delhi as an attractive market for rental income generation.
Diverse rental options are available across all BHK configurations, from Studio to 6+ BHK, catering to a wide range of tenants.
Showrooms and shops command high average rental rates (₹173 per sq ft and ₹154 per sq ft respectively), indicating robust commercial rental demand.
Under Construction properties are showing a positive 13.11% price increase, reflecting confidence in ongoing developments.
propertyRatesAiData.rentalTrendsByBHK: Delhi's residential rental market offers a broad spectrum of options, with average monthly rents varying significantly by unit type. Studio apartments average ₹16,479 per month, while 1 BHK units are priced slightly higher at ₹20,187 per month. As space increases, so does the rent, with 2 BHK apartments commanding ₹32,711 per month and 3 BHKs reaching ₹78,691 per month. Larger residential apartments, such as 4 BHKs and 5 BHKs, are available for ₹2.03 Lakh and ₹4.06 Lakh per month respectively, with 6+ BHK units topping the charts at ₹5.11 Lakh per month.
propertyRatesAiData.rentalTrendsbyLocation: The rental market across Delhi's micromarkets shows diverse pricing and performance. Central Delhi leads with the highest average rental rate of ₹47 per sq ft, experiencing a 4.44% increase. South Delhi follows at ₹42 per sq ft, though it saw a slight decrease of -2.33%. Dwarka and North Delhi both average ₹30 per sq ft, with Dwarka showing a positive 11.11% change. West Delhi offers rentals at ₹31 per sq ft but recorded a -3.12% change, while East Delhi presents the most affordable option at ₹26 per sq ft with a -7.14% change.
propertyRatesAiData.rentalTrendsByPropertyType: Delhi's rental property types showcase varying rates and trends, catering to both residential and commercial needs. Showrooms command the highest average rental rate at ₹173 per sq ft, demonstrating a strong 19.31% increase. Shops follow closely at ₹154 per sq ft with a 2.67% rise. Office spaces are available at ₹75 per sq ft, showing a healthy 10.29% growth. Residential options include villas at ₹53 per sq ft with a 15.22% increase, and apartments, the most common type, averaging ₹37 per sq ft, though they have seen a -5.13% change.
propertyRatesAiData.rentalTrendsByTopProjects: Several projects in Delhi stand out in the rental market, primarily in South Delhi. Boutique Residential Apartments A-10 in Gulmohar Park commands ₹94 per sq ft, while Boutique Residential Apartments N-136 in Panchsheel Park averages ₹90 per sq ft with a 1.12% increase. Boutique Residential Apartments C-253 in Defence Colony is priced at ₹85 per sq ft. Other notable projects include Vijay Niketan RWA in Naraina, West Delhi, at ₹83 per sq ft, and Boutique Residential Apartments S-423 in Greater Kailash I, South Delhi, offering rentals at ₹80 per sq ft.
